title: Browser / Web Tools
description: JSON Schema definitions for browser and web automation tools available to the ideas-brillantes assistant. Provides programmatic control of a headless or visible browser session: navigation, clicking, typing, content extraction, screenshots, scrolling, and waiting for conditions.

tools.name: navigate_url
tools.description: Navigate the browser to a given URL and wait for the page to load.

tools.name: web_click
tools.description: Click a web element identified by a CSS selector or XPath expression.

selector.description: CSS selector or XPath expression identifying the element to click (e.g. '#submit-btn', '//button[text()="OK"]').

tools.name: web_type
tools.description: Type text into a web input element identified by a CSS selector or XPath expression.

selector.description: CSS selector or XPath expression identifying the input element.

text.description: Text to type into the element.

tools.name: web_extract
tools.description: Extract text or structured data from the current page, optionally scoped to a CSS selector.

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope extraction to a specific element. If omitted, the full page body is used.

format.description: Output format for the extracted content. 'text' returns plain text, 'html' returns raw HTML, 'markdown' converts HTML to Markdown, 'json' attempts to parse structured data.

tools.name: web_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the current browser viewport.

path.description: Optional file path to save the screenshot image (e.g. '/tmp/page.png'). If omitted, a temporary path is used and returned.

tools.name: web_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the current page or a specific element up, down, left, or right.

amount.description: Number of pixels to scroll. Defaults to 300.

tools.name: web_wait
tools.description: Pause execution until a specified condition is met or a timeout is reached.

condition.description: Condition to wait for. Accepts a CSS selector (waits for element to appear), a URL pattern, or one of the special strings 'load' (DOMContentLoaded) or 'networkidle' (network quiet).

timeout.description: Maximum number of seconds to wait before raising a timeout error. Defaults to 10.
